nodes.label: Agent Admin instructions + capabilities architecture — a general-purpose foundation, not a single textarea/flat config
nodes.justify: grouping — the root; subsystems are the generic entry-list primitive, the instructions layer built on it, the capabilities layer built on it, persistence/live-apply integration, and the migration from the currently-shipped v1

nodes.label: generic ordered-entry-list primitive — the shared foundation both panes instantiate
nodes.justify: grouping — one typed, ordered, toggleable list model + its UI + its authoring form, reused everywhere rather than rebuilt per kind

nodes.label: entry data model — id/label/content-or-config/order/enabled/builtin-vs-custom, parameterized by a `kind`
list UI — renders a kind's entries in order with per-entry edit/enable-disable affordances
custom-entry authoring form — one shared form producing a new entry of a given kind, fail-closed validated
instructions layer — an instance of the generic list, kind="prompt-section"
nodes.justify: grouping — the structured system prompt, not a single free-text field

nodes.label: seeded default sections — Foundation / Personality / Critical Items, built-in (toggle-off, not deletable)
prompt composer — concatenates the ENABLED sections, in their defined order, into the one final system-prompt string
capabilities layer — four instances of the generic list, kind=skill|workflow|resource|tool
nodes.justify: grouping — the settings pane's toggleable capability lists

nodes.label: persistence + live-apply integration
nodes.justify: grouping — extends the already-shipped real-persistence SettingsStore work, does not replace it

nodes.label: store schema extension — persists section content + all 4 capability lists' toggle states + custom entries
turn-loop integration — the stub reply reads the COMPOSED prompt + the enabled-capabilities snapshot, not the old flat single-key read
migration from the currently-shipped v1 build
nodes.justify: grouping — what changes/is preserved from the already-reviewed, already-shipped agent-admin

nodes.label: replace the single native-textarea prompts pane with the section-list editor
disposition of the existing flat agent config (name/model/temperature/toolsEnabled) — folded in, not silently discarded

actions.label: model a named, ordered, toggleable entry within a typed list — the ONE generic shape every section/capability reuses
render a typed list's entries in order with per-entry enable/disable + edit affordances
author a new custom entry of a given kind through one shared form
seed built-in default sections (Foundation/Personality/Critical Items) as non-deletable, toggle-off-only entries
compose the final system-prompt string from the enabled sections, in their defined order
instantiate the generic list once per capability kind, each independently toggleable
extend the store schema to persist section content + all 4 capability lists' toggle states and custom entries
feed the turn loop the composed prompt + the enabled-capabilities snapshot
fold the existing flat agent config into this architecture without silently discarding it
validate a custom entry before admission (name required, no duplicate id) — fail-closed
extend the section/capability KIND taxonomy without a code change to the generic primitive itself
replace the single free-text prompt field with the section-list editor

edges.why: the list UI renders instances of the entry model — the model must exist first
the authoring form produces a new instance of the entry model
seeded sections are instances of the generic entry model
the Skills list is an instance of the generic entry model
the Workflows list is an instance of the generic entry model
the Resources list is an instance of the generic entry model
the Tools list is an instance of the generic entry model
the composer concatenates sections that must already exist
the turn loop reads the composed prompt string
the turn loop reads the enabled-skills snapshot
the turn loop reads the enabled-workflows snapshot
the turn loop reads the enabled-resources snapshot
the turn loop reads the enabled-tools snapshot
the store schema persists instances of the entry model; the model must be defined first
migrating away from the old single textarea only makes sense once the new section editor + composer are ready to replace it
